mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: Specify the collector configuration in an external ConfigMap
mission.description: Specify the collector configuration in an external ConfigMap. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry-operator deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry-operator version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
helm list -n opentelemetry-operator 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry-operator installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
### Component(s)

collector

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When changing the configuration of a collector, I have no option for a controlled rollout / rollback process in the case of an error.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Specify the collector configuration in an external ConfigMap
steps.description:
<Describe what has changed.>

From the issue:

> When changing the configuration of a collector, I have no option for a controlled rollout / rollback process in the case of an error. Once the new configuration is applied to my OpenTelemetryCollector resource, the old configuration is overwritten and all running collector pods now see the new configuration.
> 
> This makes it difficult to control the rollout process, as any pod that restarts for any reason will load with the new configuration.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade opentelemetry-operator to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade opentelemetry-operator:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade opentelemetry-operator opentelemetry-operator/opentelemetry-operator --namespace opentelemetry-operator
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator
helm list -n opentelemetry-operator
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Specify the collector configuration in an external ConfigMap" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
<Describe what has changed.>

From the issue:

> When changing the configuration of a collector, I have no option for a controlled rollout / rollback process in the case of an error. Once the new configuration is applied to my OpenTelemetryCollector resource, the old configuration is overwritten and all running collector pods now see the new configuration.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
